reachability_artifacts: built sworn ship and sworn mark-shipped runs against one temporary deployed-release history proving read-only readiness, post-deployment mutation, idempotent re-gating, and paired exit behavior
release_benefit: Operators can prove readiness before cutover and record deployment afterward without conflating or weakening either fact.

positive_scenarios: Ship is read-only and mark-shipped records only an already-deployed release whose evidence remains fully valid.
negative_scenarios: A pre-cutover check never marks deployment, and stale readiness or a deploy commit without the release never changes terminal state.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Separating safe-to-deploy from deployed keeps release history truthful while retaining one conservative evidence gate.
